_meta.description: Funding and support programs specifically targeting underrepresented entrepreneurs: immigrants and newcomers, women, Black entrepreneurs, Indigenous entrepreneurs, and youth. These are often the least-known but most impactful programs for the target personas.

plain_language_summary: Several funding programs and support organizations specifically target entrepreneurs from underrepresented groups — immigrants, women, Black Canadians, Indigenous peoples, and youth. These are often less competitive than general programs because fewer people apply, making them high-value opportunities.

programs.name_en: PACO — Programme d'accompagnement des communautés ethnoculturelles
programs.name_fr: PACO — Programme d'accompagnement des communautés ethnoculturelles
programs.plain_language: PACO provides business support, mentorship, and micro-financing specifically for immigrant entrepreneurs in Montreal. One of the best-kept secrets for newcomers.

immigration_status.note: Some programs open to work permit holders — verify

programs.languages: French, English, Spanish, Arabic, Creole, and others

programs.name_fr: Stratégie pour les femmes en entrepreneuriat
programs.plain_language: Federal program providing grants and ecosystem support for women entrepreneurs. Delivered through partner organizations across Canada including in Quebec.

amount.note: Varies by partner organization and program stream — from $5,000 to $100,000+

programs.how_to_access: Through local partner organizations — in Montreal: CEED Centre, Women in Biz Network, etc.

montreal_partners.name: CEED Centre
montreal_partners.note: Free business support and financing for women entrepreneurs in Montreal

montreal_partners.name: Réseau des Femmes d'affaires du Québec

programs.name_en: CEED Centre — Centre for Entrepreneurship Education and Development
programs.plain_language: CEED provides micro-loans and free business coaching specifically for women entrepreneurs in Montreal. One of the most accessible programs for women starting a micro-business.

amount.note: Flexible micro-loans with low interest rates for women who may not qualify for bank financing

immigration_status.note: More flexible than some federal programs — verify with CEED

eligibility.credit: Works with women who have limited or imperfect credit

programs.name_fr: Programme national pour l'entrepreneuriat des Canadiens noirs
programs.plain_language: Federal program providing loans, mentorship, and capacity building for Black Canadian entrepreneurs. Delivered through Black-led community organizations.

amount.note: Amount varies by stream and partner organization

montreal_delivery_partners.name: Groupe 3737
montreal_delivery_partners.note: Major Black entrepreneurship hub in Montreal

programs.plain_language: Montreal's major Black entrepreneurship hub — offers workspace, mentorship, funding access, and a strong community network. Located in Saint-Michel.

amount.note: Various programs — contact directly for current offerings

programs.plain_language: Quebec's immigration ministry (MIFI) provides support and referrals for immigrant entrepreneurs. Not direct funding, but connects you with the right programs and francization support.

immigration_status.note: Some services for recent arrivals regardless of status

programs.plain_language: Local youth employment centres across Quebec offer free entrepreneurship support for young people aged 16-35. Each borough in Montreal has its own CJE. Free business coaching, referrals, and sometimes small startup grants.

amount.note: Varies by CJE — some offer small startup grants or interest-free loans of $1,000-$5,000

immigration_status.eligible: All — including work permit holders and new arrivals
immigration_status.note: Very inclusive — one of few programs open to most immigration statuses

programs.montreal_offices: Each borough has a CJE — find yours at https://www.cjereseau.org
programs.why_underused: Many young immigrants and newcomers don't know CJEs serve them regardless of immigration status

programs.plain_language: YES Montréal is arguably the most valuable free resource for any entrepreneur in Montreal. Free one-on-one business coaching, workshops, job search help, and connections to funding. Serves everyone but especially strong for youth and newcomers.

eligibility.age: Primarily 18-40, but services for all ages

immigration_status.note: Serves newcomers, refugees, work permit holders, everyone

programs.languages: English, French, Spanish, and others through referral

note: Verify program availability and amounts directly — demographic-targeted programs update frequently
